results.possibleIdentity: Eric Kagan was a computer programmer and technical professional active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by the Bendix Corporation at their facility in Edgewater, New Jersey. Kagan was a member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a non-profit organization founded by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to facilitate collaborations between artists, engineers, and scientists. His professional details, including his employment at Bendix and expertise in computer programming, were documented in the E.A.T. Technical Service records, which functioned as a directory to match technical experts with artists seeking assistance for experimental projects. He is likely the same Eric Kagan who graduated from West Orange High School in New Jersey in 1960 and Columbia University in 1966, where he maintained an interest in computer science and networking.
